questions.question: Does _init_backend use _emit_state_changed(server, 'error', ...) instead of MessageType.ERROR?
questions.actionable_check: grep -n 'MessageType.ERROR' textbrush/ipc/handler.py and verify line ~870 is gone; grep -n '_emit_state_changed' textbrush/ipc/handler.py and verify 'error' state is emitted in _init_backend exception handler
evidence_required: grep output showing no MessageType.ERROR in _init_backend
grep output showing _emit_state_changed call with state='error' in _init_backend area
questions.pass_criteria: MessageType.ERROR removed from _init_backend; _emit_state_changed called with state='error', fatal=True in its place

questions.question: Does the worker error check in _start_image_delivery use _emit_state_changed instead of MessageType.ERROR?
questions.actionable_check: Read handler.py lines around _start_image_delivery worker_error check; verify MessageType.ERROR is replaced with _emit_state_changed
evidence_required: code snippet showing the worker_error handler uses _emit_state_changed
questions.pass_criteria: _emit_state_changed(server, 'error', message=str(worker_error), fatal=True) called for worker errors

questions.question: Are all remaining MessageType.ERROR usages using fatal=False (non-fatal operational errors only)?
evidence_required: grep output showing all remaining MessageType.ERROR lines have fatal=False
questions.pass_criteria: Every remaining MessageType.ERROR call uses fatal=False

questions.question: Is test_init_backend_failure_emits_error_state (renamed from test_init_backend_failure_sends_fatal_error) correct and passing?
questions.actionable_check: Run python -m pytest tests/test_ipc_handler.py::TestInitBackend::test_init_backend_failure_emits_error_state -v and read the test body
evidence_required: test body showing it asserts MessageType.STATE_CHANGED with state='error'
questions.pass_criteria: Test asserts call_args.type == MessageType.STATE_CHANGED, payload['state'] == 'error', payload['fatal'] is True, 'Model load failed' in payload['message']

questions.question: Is the unused ErrorEvent import removed from _start_image_delivery?
questions.actionable_check: grep -n 'ErrorEvent' textbrush/ipc/handler.py near _start_image_delivery function; run ruff check on handler.py
evidence_required: ruff check output showing no F401 errors
questions.pass_criteria: ruff reports 'All checks passed!' for handler.py

mandatory_questions.question: Do all 61 tests in test_ipc_handler.py still pass?
Does the overall test suite have no new failures compared to baseline (still 643 passing, 24 pre-existing torch failures)?
evidence_required: test summary showing same or better passing count
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: 643+ tests pass, no new failures beyond the 24 pre-existing torch failures
